problem text: You throw three unbiased dice. What is the probability that the greatest number rolled is a $4$?
problem solution: A direct computation method is feasible, but a more theoretical method is presented here. For the highest number to be precisely four, it must be no more than four, but greater than three. That is: $P(\textrm{max} = 4) = P(\textrm{max} \leq 4) - P(\textrm{max} \leq 3)$ $= \frac{4}{6} \times \frac{4}{6} \times \frac{4}{6} - \frac{3}{6} \times \frac{3}{6} \times \frac{3}{6}$ $= \frac{37}{216}$
problem hint: For the highest number to be exactly $4$, it must be no more than $4$, but not less than or equal to $3$.
